atten3.ptxt: When installing parts, coat the parts indicated by arrows with power steering fluid, molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease, MP grease or silicon grease (See page ).

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with power steering fluid and install it to the steering rack.
Expand a new power piston oil seal with your fingers.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to expand the oil seal excessively.

s2.ptxt: Coat the power piston oil seal with power steering fluid.
Install the power piston oil seal to the steering rack, and adjust it with your fingers.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CYLINDER TUBE OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Apply power steering fluid to the lip of a new power steering cylinder tube oil seal.
Install the power steering cylinder tube oil seal to the rack housing at a slant.
Using SST, insert the power steering cylinder tube oil seal by pushing the SST in by hand until the oil seal passes the 2 ports.

item.ptxt: Ensure that the power steering cylinder tube oil seal is installed in the correct direction as shown in the illustration.

Install the power steering cylinder tube oil seal at an angle of approximately 15° so that the lowermost part comes to the point, indicated by the arrow in the illustration, to prevent damage to the side of the oil seal when it passes the 2 ports.
Do not turn the SST when inserting the oil seal.

s2.ptxt: Install SST (09631-00200) to SST (09951-07360) upside down.
Using SST, level the power steering cylinder tube oil seal by pushing the SST in by hand. Using SST and a press, install the oil seal.
Apply molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease to the rack teeth ends.
Connect SST to the power steering rack sub-assembly.
Coat SST with power steering fluid.
Install the power steering rack sub-assembly to the power steering rack housing.
Remove SST.
Using SST and a press, install the rack bushing oil seal to the power steering rack bushing.

atten3.ptxt: Ensure that the rack bushing oil seal is installed in the correct direction as shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Coat a new O-ring with power steering fluid and install it to the rack bushing.
Coat the rack bushing oil seal lip with power steering fluid.
Install the power steering rack bushing to the power steering rack housing.

atten3.ptxt: Do not damage the rack bushing oil seal.

atten4.ptxt: Wrap protective tape around the end of the steering rack in order to prevent damage to the rack bushing oil seal.

s2.ptxt: Install the cylinder end stopper to the power steering rack housing.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL CYLINDER END STOPPER HOLE SNAP RING

s2.ptxt: Using needle nose pliers, install the cylinder end stopper hole snap ring to the power steering rack housing.

item.ptxt: Make sure that the cylinder end stopper hole snap ring is securely installed in the power steering rack housing groove.
Make sure that the cylinder end stopper hole snap ring claws are aligned with the cylinder end stopper notch.

s2.ptxt: Install SST to the power steering rack housing.
Apply a vacuum of 53 kPa (398 mmHg, 15.65 in.Hg) for approximately 30 seconds.
Check that there is no change in the vacuum pressure.
If there is a change in the vacuum pressure, check the installation of the oil seals.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE UPPER OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Coat a new power steering control valve upper oil seal lip with power steering fluid.
Using SST and a press, press in the power steering control valve upper oil seal.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the power steering control valve upper oil seal is installed in the correct direction as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE UPPER BEARING

s2.ptxt: Coat the power steering control valve upper bearing with molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease.
Using SST and a press, press in the power steering control valve upper bearing.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that the power steering control valve upper bearing is installed in the correct direction as shown in the illustration.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE SPACER

s2.ptxt: Expand 4 new power steering control valve spacers with your fingers.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to expand the power steering control valve spacers excessively.

s2.ptxt: Coat the 4 power steering control valve spacers with power steering fluid.
Install the 4 power steering control valve spacers to the control valve, and adjust them with your fingers.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE SUB-ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Coat the power steering control valve sub-assembly with power steering fluid.
Install the power steering control valve sub-assembly into the control valve housing sub-assembly.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the power steering control valve spacers and power steering control valve upper oil seal lip.

atten4.ptxt: Wrap protective tape around the spline of the power steering control valve sub-assembly in order to prevent damage to the oil seal.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE LOWER OIL SEAL

s2.ptxt: Coat a new power steering control valve lower oil seal lip with power steering fluid.
Using SST and a press, install the power steering control valve lower oil seal to the control valve assembly.

item.ptxt: Make sure that the power steering control valve lower oil seal is installed in the correct direction as shown in the illustration.
Do not damage the power steering control valve lower oil seal.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL POWER STEERING CONTROL VALVE ASSEMBLY

s2.ptxt: Install the power steering control valve assembly and a new gasket to the power steering rack housing with the 2 bolts.
Using SST, hold the control valve shaft and install a new nut.
Apply sealant to 2 or 3 threads of the rack housing cap.

specitem.ptxt: TOYOTA genuine adhesive 1344, THREE BOND 1344 or equivalent

s2.ptxt: Using a socket wrench (27 mm (1.06 in.)), install the rack housing cap.
Using a punch and a hammer, stake the rack housing cap and the power steering rack housing.
Coat a new O-ring with molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease and install it to the rack guide spring cap.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that no foreign matter, such as aluminum dust, is on the power steering rack housing and rack guide spring cap.

s2.ptxt: Apply molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease to the rack guide base and rack guide.
Install the rack guide base to the rack guide.
Apply molybdenum disulfide lithium base grease to the compression spring and the contact surface of the rack guide sub-assembly and the power steering rack.
Install the compression spring and rack guide sub-assembly to the power steering rack housing.
Using a straight hexagon wrench (24 mm (0.94 in.)), install the rack guide spring cap to the power steering rack housing.
Using SST, turn the control valve and measure the turning torque.
Put matchmarks <a> on the power steering rack housing and the rack guide spring cap.
Put matchmarks <b> 30° counterclockwise from matchmarks <a> on the power steering rack housing.

Using a straight hexagon wrench (24 mm (0.94 in.)), loosen the rack guide spring cap until matchmarks <b> and matchmarks <a> on the rack guide spring cap are aligned. Measure the rotating torque required when loosening the rack guide spring cap.
If the rotating torque required when loosening is below the specified value, perform the following procedure to achieve the specified value.

s3.ptxt: Using a straight hexagon wrench (24 mm (0.94 in.)), re-tighten the rack guide spring cap.
Using a punch and hammer, stake the power steering rack housing and rack guide spring cap at 3 equally spaced positions.

atten4.ptxt: Tap the power steering rack housing and rack guide spring cap strongly and repeatedly to stake them securely.

s3.ptxt: Using a straight hexagon wrench (24 mm (0.94 in.)), loosen the rack guide spring cap until matchmarks <b> and matchmarks <a> on the rack guide spring cap are aligned. Measure the rotating torque required when loosening the rack guide spring cap.

s2.ptxt: Temporarily install the 2 steering rack ends to the power steering rack.

atten3.ptxt: Do not fully turn the power steering rack without the steering rack ends as it may damage the oil seal in the rack housing.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, fully turn the power steering rack right and left 10 times to settle it.
Using SST, turn the control valve and measure the preload.
If the preload exceeds the specified value, proceed to INSTALL RACK GUIDE (See page ).
Remove the 2 steering rack ends from the power steering rack.
Apply MP grease around the control valve shaft as shown in the illustration.
Wrap vinyl tape around the spline of the control valve.
Install the dust cover to the control valve assembly.
Install 2 new claw washers.

atten4.ptxt: Align the claws of the claw washer with the steering rack grooves.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, install the steering rack end sub-assembly (LH side).

item.ptxt: Use SST 09922-10010 as shown in the illustration.
Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 345 mm (13.58 in.)
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to the torque wrench.

atten4.ptxt: Using SST, hold the steering rack and install the steering rack end sub-assembly (LH side).

s2.ptxt: Using SST, install the steering rack end sub-assembly (RH side).

item.ptxt: Use SST 09922-10010 as shown in the illustration.
Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 345 mm (13.58 in.)
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to the torque wrench.

atten4.ptxt: Using SST, hold the steering rack end sub-assembly (LH side) and install the steering rack end sub-assembly (RH side).

s2.ptxt: Using a brass bar and a hammer, stake the 2 claw washers.

atten3.ptxt: Avoid any impact to the steering rack.

s2.ptxt: Ensure that the holes of the rack ends are not clogged with grease.

atten4.ptxt: If the hole is clogged, the pressure inside the boot will change after it is assembled and the steering wheel is turned.

s2.ptxt: Apply silicon grease to the inside of the small opening of the No. 2 steering rack boot.
Install the No. 2 steering rack boot to the groove on the rack housing.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage or twist the No. 2 steering rack boot.

atten4.ptxt: Perform the same procedure as for the No. 2 steering rack boot.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL NO. 2 STEERING RACK BOOT CLAMP

s2.ptxt: Using SST, tighten the 2 No. 2 steering rack boot clamps as shown in the illustration.

atten3.ptxt: Be careful not to damage the No. 2 steering rack boot.

s2.ptxt: Using pliers, install the 2 steering rack boot clips.

atten3.ptxt: Make sure that each clip claw is positioned within the area shown in the illustration.

s2.ptxt: Using SST, turn the pinion and check that the rack boots expand and contract smoothly.
Install the lock nut and the tie rod assembly LH to the steering rack end sub-assembly until the matchmarks are aligned.

atten4.ptxt: After adjusting toe-in, torque the lock nut.
Perform the same procedure as for the LH side.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING RIGHT TURN PRESSURE TUBE

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new O-rings with power steering fluid and install them to the steering right turn pressure tube.
Using SST, install the right turn pressure tube to the power steering link assembly.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to the torque wrench.

s-1.ptxt: INSTALL STEERING LEFT TURN PRESSURE TUBE

s2.ptxt: Coat 2 new O-rings with power steering fluid and install them to the steering left turn pressure tube.
Using SST, install the left turn pressure tube to the power steering link assembly.

item.ptxt: Use a torque wrench with a fulcrum length of 250 mm (9.84 in.).
This torque value is effective when SST is parallel to the torque wrench.
